data.description:

Approach 1: Cardinal Health Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow model projects the cash Cardinal Health could generate in the future, then discounts those cash flows back to today to estimate what the business might be worth right now.

Cardinal Health currently reports last twelve month Free Cash Flow of about $5.52b. Using a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity model, analysts and extrapolations feed in annual cash flow projections, including an estimated $4.75b in Free Cash Flow by 2030. Beyond the explicit analyst horizon, Simply Wall St extends the projections further out to 2035 using gradual growth assumptions, then discounts each of those future cash flows back to today.

On this basis, the DCF model suggests an intrinsic value of about $525.18 per share, which compares to the recent share price of around $200.55. That gap implies a 61.8% discount, so the stock screens as materially undervalued using this method alone.

Result: UNDERVALUED

For a profitable company like Cardinal Health, the P/E ratio is a straightforward way to relate what you pay per share to the earnings the business is currently generating. Investors generally accept that higher growth expectations or lower perceived risk can support a higher "normal" P/E, while slower growth or higher risk tend to align with a lower multiple.

Cardinal Health currently trades on a P/E of 28.40x. That sits above the Healthcare industry average of 23.32x and the peer average of 24.14x. On simple comparisons, the shares look more expensive than many sector peers.

data.title: Results: Northern Trust Corporation Exceeded Expectations And The Consensus Has Updated Its Estimates
data.description:
It's been a good week for Northern Trust Corporation (NASDAQ:NTRS) shareholders, because the company has just released its latest quarterly results, and the shares gained 7.5% to US$168. It looks to have been a decent result overall - while revenue fell marginally short of analyst estimates at US$2.2b, statutory earnings beat expectations by a notable 16%, coming in at US$2.71 per share. Following the result, the analysts have updated their earnings model, and it would be good to know whether they think there's been a strong change in the company's prospects, or if it's business as usual. With this in mind, we've gathered the latest statutory forecasts to see what the analysts are expecting for next year.

Following the latest results, Northern Trust's 13 analysts are now forecasting revenues of US$8.84b in 2026. This would be a reasonable 5.7% improvement in revenue compared to the last 12 months. Per-share earnings are expected to step up 14% to US$11.13. In the lead-up to this report, the analysts had been modelling revenues of US$8.80b and earnings per share (EPS) of US$10.57 in 2026. The analysts seems to have become more bullish on the business, judging by their new earnings per share estimates.

Check out our latest analysis for Northern Trust

The consensus price target rose 8.9% to US$167, suggesting that higher earnings estimates flow through to the stock's valuation as well. It could also be instructive to look at the range of analyst estimates, to evaluate how different the outlier opinions are from the mean. Currently, the most bullish analyst values Northern Trust at US$190 per share, while the most bearish prices it at US$130. As you can see, analysts are not all in agreement on the stock's future, but the range of estimates is still reasonably narrow, which could suggest that the outcome is not totally unpredictable.

Looking at the bigger picture now, one of the ways we can make sense of these forecasts is to see how they measure up against both past performance and industry growth estimates. The analysts are definitely expecting Northern Trust's growth to accelerate, with the forecast 7.7% annualised growth to the end of 2026 ranking favourably alongside historical growth of 6.3% per annum over the past five years. By contrast, our data suggests that other companies (with analyst coverage) in a similar industry are forecast to grow their revenue at 5.4% per year. Factoring in the forecast acceleration in revenue, it's pretty clear that Northern Trust is expected to grow much faster than its industry.

The Bottom Line

The most important thing here is that the analysts upgraded their earnings per share estimates, suggesting that there has been a clear increase in optimism towards Northern Trust following these results. Happily, there were no major changes to revenue forecasts, with the business still expected to grow faster than the wider industry. We note an upgrade to the price target, suggesting that the analysts believes the intrinsic value of the business is likely to improve over time.

Keeping that in mind, we still think that the longer term trajectory of the business is much more important for investors to consider. We have estimates - from multiple Northern Trust analysts - going out to 2028, and you can see them free on our platform here.

data.title: Have Insiders Sold Citigroup Shares Recently?
data.description:
Some Citigroup Inc. (NYSE:C) shareholders may be a little concerned to see that insider Edward Skyler recently sold a substantial US$3.3m worth of stock at a price of US$131 per share. That's a big disposal, and it decreased their holding size by 12%, which is notable but not too bad.

We've found 21 US stocks that are forecast to pay a dividend yield of over 6% next year. See the full list for free.

Advertisement

The Last 12 Months Of Insider Transactions At Citigroup

Over the last year, we can see that the biggest insider sale was by the Head of International, Ernesto Cantu, for US$7.5m worth of shares, at about US$111 per share. So it's clear an insider wanted to take some cash off the table, even below the current price of US$130. When an insider sells below the current price, it suggests that they considered that lower price to be fair. That makes us wonder what they think of the (higher) recent valuation. However, while insider selling is sometimes discouraging, it's only a weak signal. We note that the biggest single sale was 55% of Ernesto Cantu's holding.

Citigroup insiders didn't buy any shares over the last year. The chart below shows insider transactions (by companies and individuals) over the last year. If you want to know exactly who sold, for how much, and when, simply click on the graph below!

View our latest analysis for Citigroup

I will like Citigroup better if I see some big insider buys. While we wait, check out this free list of undervalued and small cap stocks with considerable, recent, insider buying.

Does Citigroup Boast High Insider Ownership?

Many investors like to check how much of a company is owned by insiders. Usually, the higher the insider ownership, the more likely it is that insiders will be incentivised to build the company for the long term. Citigroup insiders own about US$505m worth of shares (which is 0.2% of the company). Most shareholders would be happy to see this sort of insider ownership, since it suggests that management incentives are well aligned with other shareholders.

So What Do The Citigroup Insider Transactions Indicate?

Insiders haven't bought Citigroup stock in the last three months, but there was some selling. And there weren't any purchases to give us comfort, over the last year. But it is good to see that Citigroup is growing earnings. While insiders do own a lot of shares in the company (which is good), our analysis of their transactions doesn't make us feel confident about the company. So while it's helpful to know what insiders are doing in terms of buying or selling, it's also helpful to know the risks that a particular company is facing. But note: Citigroup may not be the best stock to buy. So take a peek at this free list of interesting companies with high ROE and low debt.

Approach 1: nVent Electric Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow model takes estimates of a company’s future cash flows, then discounts them back to today to get an implied value per share. For nVent Electric, the model used is a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach, based on cash flow projections expressed in US$.

The company’s last twelve month free cash flow is about $370.4 million. Analysts provide detailed estimates for the next few years, and Simply Wall St then extrapolates these further out. Under this framework, projected free cash flow for 2028 is $879.4 million, with additional estimates running through to 2035, all discounted back using the DCF approach.

Putting all those discounted cash flows together, the model arrives at an estimated intrinsic value of $87.45 per share. Compared with the recent share price of $140.13, this implies the stock is 60.2% overvalued on this DCF view.

Approach 2: nVent Electric Price vs Earnings

For a profitable company, the P/E ratio is a useful way to see how much you are paying for each dollar of earnings. It reflects what investors are willing to pay today for current profits, while also embedding expectations about future growth and the risk of those earnings.

Higher growth and lower perceived risk usually support a higher “normal” P/E, while slower growth or higher uncertainty tend to justify a lower one. nVent Electric is currently trading on a P/E of 52.88x. That is above the Electrical industry average of 33.86x and above the peer average of 47.73x.

data.title: Orrstown Financial Services (ORRF) Net Margin Surge Reinforces Bullish Profitability Narrative
data.description:
Orrstown Financial Services (ORRF) opened 2026 with Q1 revenue of US$64.2 million and basic EPS of US$1.13, supported by quarterly net income of US$21.8 million and a trailing twelve month EPS of US$4.40 that sits against a 168% year over year earnings lift and a net profit margin of 33.1% versus 15.5% a year earlier. Over the past year, revenue on a trailing basis has moved from US$176.1 million in Q4 2024 to US$255.4 million in Q1 2026, while quarterly EPS has ranged from US$0.72 in Q4 2024 to US$1.14 in Q3 2025 and US$1.13 in the latest quarter. This sets up a results picture where profitability and margins are front and center for investors weighing the new numbers against forward expectations.

See our full analysis for Orrstown Financial Services.

With the headline figures on the table, the next step is to see how these results line up with the widely held narratives about Orrstown Financial Services and where the latest earnings might challenge those views.

See what the community is saying about Orrstown Financial Services

Advertisement

Net interest and cost ratios set the tone

  • Q1 2026 net interest margin was 3.9% with a cost to income ratio of 56.9%, compared with a trailing twelve month net margin of 33.1% and earnings growth of 168% over the past year.
  • Analysts' consensus view highlights efficiency and profitability initiatives, and this set of margins partly speaks to that:
    • Consensus commentary points to process improvements and reduced merger related and consulting expenses as a way to improve cost control, while the current 56.9% cost to income ratio shows how much of each revenue dollar is still going to operating costs.
    • The 3.9% net interest margin in Q1 alongside a 33.1% trailing net margin lines up with the idea that the core banking engine is producing solid profits. At the same time, expectations for revenue to decline about 9.9% per year highlight why analysts are cautious about how durable this profitability will be.

Loan growth meets rising non performing balances

  • Total loans reached US$4,061.3 million in Q1 2026, up from US$3,875.99 million in Q1 2025, while non performing loans moved from US$22.73 million to US$30.03 million over the same period.
  • Consensus narrative talks up a strong commercial loan pipeline and disciplined credit risk management, and these figures cut both ways:
    • The increase in total loans over the last five reported quarters supports the view that commercial and regional lending opportunities are being used, consistent with commentary about small business and suburban growth driving higher net interest income.
    • The rise in non performing loans from US$22.73 million to US$30.03 million contrasts with the thesis of declining nonaccruals, so anyone relying on the bullish credit story may want to watch whether this is a short term bump or something that keeps pressure on future provisions.
On the credit side, bulls argue that a growing loan book and regional opportunities can support long term earnings, but these loan quality trends show why it helps to read both the optimistic and cautious takes before forming a view on the story for Orrstown Financial Services.🐂 Orrstown Financial Services Bull Case

Valuation gap and forecast revenue drop

  • The shares trade at US$35.87 with a P/E of 8.3x, compared with an analyst price target of US$42.00 and a DCF fair value of US$33.09, while analysts expect revenue to decline about 9.9% per year and earnings about 0.3% per year over the next three years.
  • Bears focus on those expected revenue declines and the stock sitting above DCF fair value, and the current data provides several anchors for that view:
    • Forecast annual revenue contraction of roughly 9.9% alongside only a 0.3% annual earnings decline assumes margins stay strong. Any slip from the recent 33.1% net margin could leave less room for error than the 8.3x P/E multiple might suggest.
    • The current price of US$35.87 is higher than the US$33.09 DCF fair value and below the US$42.00 analyst target, which fits the cautious argument that the market is already factoring in part of the strong recent profitability while also reflecting concerns such as recent insider selling and the outlook for lower top line.
Skeptics point out that a low P/E only goes so far if revenue shrinks, so it can be useful to see how the more bearish analysts break down those forecasts for Orrstown Financial Services.🐻 Orrstown Financial Services Bear Case

data.title: Earnings Update: United Community Banks, Inc. (NYSE:UCB) Just Reported Its First-Quarter Results And Analysts Are Updating Their Forecasts
data.description:
Last week saw the newest first-quarter earnings release from United Community Banks, Inc. (NYSE:UCB), an important milestone in the company's journey to build a stronger business. United Community Banks reported in line with analyst predictions, delivering revenues of US$278m and statutory earnings per share of US$0.69, suggesting the business is executing well and in line with its plan. The analysts typically update their forecasts at each earnings report, and we can judge from their estimates whether their view of the company has changed or if there are any new concerns to be aware of. We thought readers would find it interesting to see the analysts latest (statutory) post-earnings forecasts for next year.

Trump has pledged to "unleash" American oil and gas and these 15 US stocks have developments that are poised to benefit.

Taking into account the latest results, the consensus forecast from United Community Banks' seven analysts is for revenues of US$1.14b in 2026. This reflects a meaningful 8.7% improvement in revenue compared to the last 12 months. Per-share earnings are expected to accumulate 6.8% to US$2.97. Yet prior to the latest earnings, the analysts had been anticipated revenues of US$1.14b and earnings per share (EPS) of US$2.98 in 2026. The consensus analysts don't seem to have seen anything in these results that would have changed their view on the business, given there's been no major change to their estimates.

View our latest analysis for United Community Banks

It will come as no surprise then, to learn that the consensus price target is largely unchanged at US$38.17. It could also be instructive to look at the range of analyst estimates, to evaluate how different the outlier opinions are from the mean. Currently, the most bullish analyst values United Community Banks at US$40.00 per share, while the most bearish prices it at US$36.00. Even so, with a relatively close grouping of estimates, it looks like the analysts are quite confident in their valuations, suggesting United Community Banks is an easy business to forecast or the the analysts are all using similar assumptions.

Another way we can view these estimates is in the context of the bigger picture, such as how the forecasts stack up against past performance, and whether forecasts are more or less bullish relative to other companies in the industry. The analysts are definitely expecting United Community Banks' growth to accelerate, with the forecast 12% annualised growth to the end of 2026 ranking favourably alongside historical growth of 7.2% per annum over the past five years. By contrast, our data suggests that other companies (with analyst coverage) in a similar industry are forecast to grow their revenue at 8.7% per year. It seems obvious that, while the growth outlook is brighter than the recent past, the analysts also expect United Community Banks to grow faster than the wider industry.

Advertisement

The Bottom Line

The most important thing to take away is that there's been no major change in sentiment, with the analysts reconfirming that the business is performing in line with their previous earnings per share estimates. Fortunately, they also reconfirmed their revenue numbers, suggesting that it's tracking in line with expectations. Additionally, our data suggests that revenue is expected to grow faster than the wider industry. There was no real change to the consensus price target, suggesting that the intrinsic value of the business has not undergone any major changes with the latest estimates.

With that said, the long-term trajectory of the company's earnings is a lot more important than next year. We have forecasts for United Community Banks going out to 2027, and you can see them free on our platform here.

However, before you get too enthused, we've discovered 1 warning sign for United Community Banks that you should be aware of.

data.title: WesBanco (WSBC) Q1 Net Profit Margin Story Tests Bullish Efficiency Narrative
data.description:
WesBanco (WSBC) opened 2026 with Q1 revenue of US$258.1 million and net income of US$84.4 million, translating to EPS of US$0.88, set against a trailing 12 month EPS of US$3.12 on revenue of US$1.04 billion. Over recent periods, revenue has ranged from US$123.4 million in Q1 2025 to around US$262.5 million in Q4 2025, while quarterly EPS moved from a loss of US$0.15 in Q1 2025 to US$0.81 in Q4 2025 and US$0.88 in the latest quarter. This gives investors a clearer earnings run-rate to assess alongside the current update. With a trailing 12 month net profit margin of 28.8% compared with 17% the prior year, the earnings release focuses attention on how sustainably WesBanco is converting its revenue line into bottom line results.

See our full analysis for WesBanco.

With the headline numbers on the table, the next step is to see how this earnings profile lines up with the widely followed bull, bear, and consensus narratives around WesBanco and where those stories might need updating.

See what the community is saying about WesBanco

Advertisement

Margins and Costs Point to Efficiency Story

  • For Q1 2026, WesBanco reported a net interest margin of 3.57% and a cost to income ratio of 52.54%. The trailing 12 month net profit margin sits at 28.8% compared with 17% the prior year.
  • Analysts' consensus view stresses improving profitability and operational efficiency. These margin figures both support that idea and provide a way to test how far it can go over time.
    • The consensus narrative highlights a 10 percentage point efficiency improvement year over year. The current 52.54% cost to income ratio gives a concrete baseline for assessing how much room there is if that trend continues or slows.
    • A 28.8% trailing 12 month net profit margin versus 17% a year earlier is consistent with the view that tighter cost control and higher margin business lines, such as wealth and fee income, are feeding through to the bottom line.
On these numbers, bulls argue that better efficiency could be a key earnings driver if management keeps costs in check and focuses on higher margin products. 🐂 WesBanco Bull Case

Loan Book Growth Versus Rising Non Performing Loans

  • Total loans are reported at US$19.1b in Q1 2026, up from US$18.7b in Q1 2025. Non performing loans on the balance sheet data rose from US$81.5 million in Q1 2025 to US$145.0 million on a trailing 12 month basis at Q1 2026.
  • Bears focus on credit risk and geographic concentration, and the move in non performing loans gives them concrete evidence to compare against expansion plans.
    • Cautious investors flag that reliance on commercial real estate and acquired portfolios could amplify risk if payoffs or refinancing terms worsen. The step up in non performing loans to US$145.0 million is exactly the type of data they watch.
    • Total loans of around US$19.1b also indicate that WesBanco is still growing its book in markets like the Midwest and Appalachia, which bears argue could leave earnings more exposed if those regional economies soften.
Skeptics warn that credit quality metrics such as the US$145.0 million in non performing loans could become more important than loan growth if economic conditions turn. 🐻 WesBanco Bear Case

Valuation, Yield and Analyst Targets in Focus

  • With a current share price of US$33.10, WesBanco trades on a P/E of 10.7x compared with a reported US Banks industry average of 11.7x and peer average of 17.7x. The shares also carry a 4.59% dividend yield and an analyst consensus price target of US$40.25 versus the DCF fair value of about US$72.91.
  • Consensus narrative supporters point to potential upside and income, and the longer term earnings trend and below market forecast growth give investors a clear checklist to work through.
    • The gap between the current price of US$33.10, the US$40.25 analyst target and the DCF fair value of about US$72.91 means anyone leaning into the value case will also want to factor in the negative 4.3% annualized five year EPS trend.
    • A 4.59% yield, year over year earnings growth of more than 200% and forecast earnings growth of 15.6% annually provide the data behind the idea that income and recovery together could appeal to more value oriented investors.
